statement.en: Implications of the Muslim-Muslim Political Ticket in Building Muslim-Christian Relation, Equitable Representation and Peaceful Co-Existence in Niger State
statement.summary: Abstract: The main purpose of the paper is to evaluate muslim- muslim political ticket in building interfaith relations and equitable political representation in Niger State. The paper adopted a literature review design to access and discover information that is related and relevant to the paper. Christian-Muslim relations had been a global concern, and both religions originated in Palestine. This relationship continued to widen apart as time progressed under the caliphs; policies were made that were unfavorable to Christians in the process of spreading Islam. Presently, Christians and Muslims continue to manage peaceful coexistence despite differences and challenges. Historically, no Christian has served as governor under a democratic era in Niger State. The 2023 gubernatorial election un

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom is a provisional research claim (preprint, 2026-04-25) regarding political representation outcomes. It makes empirical and normative assertions about interfaith relations and electoral systems that require validation through peer review, longitudinal data, and comparative political analysis. The thesis is not yet established fact nor refuted, but remains under investigation and contestation within political science literature.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 同じ信仰の切符が、過去の代表不足と現在の不均衡のはざまで、信頼を三乗根で蝕む。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 二つの円が狭い道で触れ、その間に垂直な道が引かれる —分断と統合の緊張の幾何学。
